articles.title: PEOPLE
articles.summary: In the latest Unpack Pricing podcast [4], Metronome CEO Scott Woody talks with Nicolas Kopp, founder of Rillet (AI-native ERP, backed by Sequoia, a16z, and Iconiq) about how AI is overhauling finance operations.

articles.title: FOUR AI RESEARCH TRENDS ENTERPRISE TEAMS SHOULD WATCH IN 2026
articles.summary: Key AI research trends to watch in 2026 include continual learning, world models, orchestration, and refinement. Continual learning focuses on updating models without retraining, while world models enable AI to understand environments autonomously. Orchestration enhances efficiency by coordinating multiple tools, and refinement improves outputs iteratively through feedback.

articles.title: 'COLLEGE DROPOUT' HAS BECOME THE MOST COVETED STARTUP FOUNDER CREDENTIAL
articles.summary: The trend of valuing college dropouts as startup founders has resurged amid the AI boom, with many pitching it as a credential during Y Combinator Demo Days. Despite high-profile examples and fears of missing critical AI opportunities, most successful startup founders hold degrees. VCs prioritize traits such as commitment and social networks over dropout status, with some emphasizing the value of wisdom gained from experience.

articles.title: THE RACE TO RUN BUSINESSES AUTONOMOUSLY
articles.summary: Cofounder, by The General Intelligence Company, enables businesses to run autonomously through a network of coordinating agents that execute repeatable workflows known as Flows. The platform employs layered memory architectures to consolidate information, allowing agents to operate independently with comprehensive knowledge. This approach aims to reduce manual repetitive work and enable continuous, insightful, and autonomous business operations.

articles.title: 25 LESSONS FROM THE FIRST YEAR OF SOLO FOUNDERS
articles.summary: Solo founding is more viable than most assume. Key lessons: you can achieve significant traction alone, momentum beats co-founder searching, and early focus on the core product matters. Solo founders benefit from generous equity, contractors-to-hires, and multitasking enabled by AI. Mindset is crucial: endurance, pacing, and learning from small pivots drive success. Emotional support and realistic benchmarks prevent burnout. Fundraising, timing, and terms matter, but execution outweighs optics. Ultimately, persistence and continuous iteration define survival and growth.

articles.title: BUILD THE WHOLE PRODUCT OR LOSE THE CUSTOMER
articles.summary: Winning startups in 2026 will replace fragmented toolchains by solving an entire end-to-end problem for a tightly defined customer segment. The strongest teams will compete across categories to deliver a single, integrated solution that beats stitching together multiple incumbents on cost, speed, and experience. Square proved the model by compressing payments, hardware, and POS into one system, cutting the total cost of ownership and capturing value across the stack.

articles.title: IS COMMUNITY THE MOST OVERLOOKED EDGE IN VENTURE CAPITAL?
articles.summary: Community roles in VC are evolving beyond event planning to strategic network development, becoming essential for mapping emerging networks and unlocking investment opportunities. The need to treat community as infrastructure is driving firms to institutionalize these roles, leveraging relationships to find untapped potential before consensus forms. Measuring meaningful metrics like founders sourced and pre-empted rounds will crystallize community as a strategic discipline rather than a peripheral function.

articles.title: FRIENDWARE
articles.summary: Friendware is a macOS productivity tool that provides system-wide tab-to-complete suggestions to reduce context switching.

articles.title: CREAIBO
articles.summary: Creaibo is an AI content creation tool that helps creators develop and produce written and visual content while maintaining a consistent personal style.

articles.title: FOUNDIRE
articles.summary: Foundire is a hiring platform that automates first-round interviews using AI-driven candidate screening, interviews, and structured evaluation scorecards.

articles.title: THE CASE AGAINST DOOMERISM
articles.summary: 2026 marks the mainstream arrival of AI capabilities that feel like “magic,” making many traditional roles, like PMs, appear obsolete. Doomerism stems from outdated experiences with early AI tools, but avoiding these tools limits growth. Drawing lessons from chess, the author argues that leveraging AI enhances judgment and output, while refusal only widens the gap. Fear of dependency, not capability, is the real challenge. Participation now shapes the future; opting out leaves you behind.

articles.title: THE NAVAN IPO REMINDS US: AI IS ON FIRE. EVERYTHING ELSE IN B2B? STILL REALLY, REALLY HARD
articles.summary: Navan's stock dropped 20% on day one when it went public in Q4 last year. It is still trading 32% below its IPO price. Navan is a legitimate B2B and SaaS category leader - but it isn't AI. Simply being a SaaS company is no longer a ticket to a premium valuation, unless you're building AI infrastructure, AI models, or AI agents.

articles.title: THE NEXT THING WILL NOT BE BIG
articles.summary: Open source developers should dedicate more energy to medium- or small-scale open source projects.

articles.title: DOES VALUATION OR OWNERSHIP MATTER MORE TO A SEED VC? YES
articles.summary: Seed VCs prioritize ownership over valuation, targeting 10–15% ownership per investment, but a focus on pricing can influence portfolio strategy.

articles.title: THE TOLL OF STRESS ON STARTUP TEAMS AND ITS LINK TO FOUNDER WELL-BEING
articles.summary: Stress and burnout are significant but overlooked factors that affect startup performance.

articles.title: WHAT IT'S LIKE TO WORK AT A PROFITABLE AI STARTUP BUILT FOR THE TRADES
articles.summary: Hearth's completely automatic revenue engine allows contractors to focus on what they do best.
